# Canvas — Article Enhancement Spec
**For:** Replit Developer  
**Priority:** High — applies to all articles generated by Canvas  
**Prerequisite:** canvas-ai-content-generation-spec.md must be implemented first

---

## Overview

Articles currently link to collections but are missing four elements that 
significantly improve both conversion and AI/LLM discoverability:

1. Individual product cards with images pulled from Shopify
2. Contextual CTAs placed throughout and at the bottom of every article
3. FAQ section with structured schema markup
4. Structured data (JSON-LD) for LLM and search engine optimization

Each is detailed below with implementation instructions.

---

## Change 1 — Individual Product Cards with Shopify Images

### Why
Linking to collections is useful but loses conversion momentum. Individual 
product cards with images, names, and direct product URLs give readers 
a clear next step and give Google/LLMs specific product entities to index.

### What to build
A reusable `ProductCard` block that Canvas inserts at relevant points in 
the article body. Each card contains:

- Product image (pulled from Shopify CDN)
- Product name
- One-line product description (from Shopify product description or 
  auto-generated from the article context)
- Price
- Direct "Shop This" link to the product page URL

### Shopify API call
```
GET /admin/api/2024-01/products.json
  ?collection_id={relevant_collection_id}
  &fields=id,title,handle,images,variants,body_html
  &limit=12
```

Build a product reference helper that:
1. Takes the article's keyword cluster as input
2. Maps cluster → relevant Shopify collection(s)
3. Pulls top products from those collections
4. Returns product cards ready for injection

### Cluster → Collection mapping (starter)
```
Hikers & Outdoors        → /collections/gifts-for-hikers (or outdoors)
Travelers                → /collections/travel-gifts
Map Gifts                → /collections/city-maps, /collections/us-city-maps
Constellation            → /collections/night-sky-products
Occasion — Anniversary   → /collections/anniversary-gifts
Occasion — Father's Day  → /collections/fathers-day-gifts
Occasion — Mother's Day  → /collections/gifts-for-mom
Glassware                → /collections/wine-glasses, /collections/rocks-glasses
Corporate / Professional → /collections/corporate-gifts
```
Expand this mapping as new clusters are added to the keyword library.

### Image handling
- Use Shopify CDN URLs directly — do not re-host images
- Request the `_600x` size variant for article inline images: 
  `{image_src}?width=600`
- Always include descriptive alt text combining product name + context:
  `alt="[Product Name] — [brief descriptor, e.g. 'city map wine glass for anniversary gift']"`
- Lazy load all product images (`loading="lazy"`)

### Placement in article
- Insert 1–2 product cards after every 2–3 product recommendations 
  in the article body
- Never lead with a product card — always follow prose
- At the bottom of the article, insert a "Featured Products" grid 
  of 3–4 cards before the final CTA

### Rendered HTML structure
```html
<div class="wt-product-card">
  <a href="/products/{handle}" class="wt-product-card__link">
    <img 
      src="{shopify_cdn_url}?width=600" 
      alt="{product_name} — {descriptor}"
      loading="lazy"
      width="600"
    />
    <div class="wt-product-card__body">
      <h3 class="wt-product-card__title">{product_name}</h3>
      <p class="wt-product-card__description">{one_line_description}</p>
      <span class="wt-product-card__price">${price}</span>
      <span class="wt-product-card__cta">Shop This →</span>
    </div>
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## Change 2 — Contextual CTAs

### Why
Articles currently end without a clear next action. Readers who are 
persuaded mid-article have no conversion path. CTAs placed contextually 
(not just at the bottom) capture intent at the moment it peaks.

### CTA types to implement

**Type A — Mid-article inline CTA**  
Placed after every 2–3 product sections. Brief, contextual to what 
was just discussed. One link.

```html
<div class="wt-cta-inline">
  <p>{contextual_line}</p>
  <a href="{collection_url}" class="wt-cta-inline__btn">{button_text}</a>
</div>
```

Example output for a gifts-for-mom article:
> "Every glass is engraved with the city she calls home — or the one 
> she'll never stop missing."  
> [Browse Personalized Gifts for Mom →]

**Type B — Bottom CTA section**  
Full-width section at the end of every article before the FAQ. 
More prominent, with a headline and supporting line.

```html
<section class="wt-cta-bottom">
  <h2 class="wt-cta-bottom__headline">{headline}</h2>
  <p class="wt-cta-bottom__body">{supporting_line}</p>
  <a href="{primary_url}" class="wt-cta-bottom__btn">{primary_button}</a>
  <a href="{secondary_url}" class="wt-cta-bottom__link">{secondary_link}</a>
</section>
```

Example output:
> **Find the gift that marks the moment.**  
> Every Well Told piece is engraved to order — the city, the constellation, 
> the trail. Ships in 3–5 days.  
> [Shop Personalized Gifts] [View All Collections →]

### How Canvas generates CTA copy
Add CTA generation as a second AI call after the article body is generated. 
Pass the article topic, primary keyword, and primary collection URL.

Prompt for CTA generation:
```
Using the Well Told brand voice (warm, specific, story-driven — not salesy), 
write two CTAs for this article:

Article topic: {primary_keyword}
Primary collection: {collection_name} at {collection_url}

CTA 1 (inline, mid-article): One sentence + one button label. 
Maximum 20 words. Lead with a specific image or place, not a product feature.

CTA 2 (bottom section): One headline (8 words max) + one supporting 
sentence (20 words max) + one primary button label + one secondary 
text link label.

Do not use: "shop now", "click here", "amazing", "perfect", 
exclamation points, or urgency language.
```

### CTA placement logic
```
Article body generated
→ Insert Type A CTA after product section 2
→ Insert Type A CTA after product section 4 (if article has 6+ sections)
→ Insert featured product grid (3–4 cards)
→ Insert Type B bottom CTA
→ Insert FAQ section (see Change 3)
→ End of article
```

---

## Change 3 — FAQ Section with Schema Markup

### Why this matters for both SEO and LLMs
FAQ sections serve three distinct purposes:

1. **Google rich results** — FAQ schema markup enables expandable Q&A 
   directly in Google search results, increasing CTR without ranking higher
2. **AI Overview inclusion** — Google's AI Overviews frequently pull 
   from FAQ-structured content because it's direct and pre-formatted 
   for answer extraction
3. **LLM citation** — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Claude all 
   favor content with clear question-and-answer structure when generating 
   responses about gift recommendations. Well Told currently has 357 AI 
   mentions — FAQ content is a direct path to growing that number

### What to build
Generate 4–6 FAQ questions per article. Questions should mirror real 
"People Also Ask" queries related to the primary keyword.

### FAQ generation prompt (second AI call)
```
Generate 5 FAQ questions and answers for an article about: {primary_keyword}

Rules:
- Questions should mirror how a real person searches Google 
  (e.g. "What is a good gift for mom who has everything?")
- Answers should be 2–4 sentences, direct, and specific
- At least 2 answers should naturally mention a Well Told product 
  type (map glassware, constellation gifts, personalized drinkware, etc.)
- Do not be salesy — answer the question genuinely first, 
  then mention the product as an example
- Questions should cover: what to give, how to choose, what makes 
  a good gift, budget considerations, and one specific Well Told angle

Format output as JSON:
[
  {"question": "...", "answer": "..."},
  {"question": "...", "answer": "..."}
]
```

### Rendered HTML
```html
<section class="wt-faq" itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/FAQPage">
  <h2>Frequently Asked Questions</h2>
  
  {for each question}
  <div class="wt-faq__item" 
       itemscope 
       itemprop="mainEntity" 
       itemtype="https://schema.org/Question">
    <h3 class="wt-faq__question" itemprop="name">
      {question}
    </h3>
    <div class="wt-faq__answer"
         itemscope
         itemprop="acceptedAnswer"
         itemtype="https://schema.org/Answer">
      <div itemprop="text">
        {answer}
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  {end for}
</section>
```

### FAQ placement
Always last section of the article — after the bottom CTA block. 
The FAQ is an SEO and LLM asset, not a conversion tool, so it lives 
at the end after all conversion opportunities have been presented.

---

## Change 4 — Structured Data (JSON-LD) for LLM and Search Optimization

### Why
Structured data tells search engines and LLMs exactly what a page is, 
who published it, and what it contains. Well Told already has strong 
AI visibility (357 mentions, 278 cited pages). Structured data 
systematizes this — every new article contributes to a growing 
machine-readable entity graph around the Well Told brand.

### Schema blocks to inject in every article's `<head>`

**Block A — Article Schema**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  "headline": "{article_title}",
  "description": "{meta_description}",
  "datePublished": "{publish_date_ISO}",
  "dateModified": "{modified_date_ISO}",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Well Told Design",
    "url": "https://welltolddesign.com"
  },
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Well Told Design",
    "url": "https://welltolddesign.com",
    "logo": {
      "@type": "ImageObject",
      "url": "https://welltolddesign.com/[logo-url]"
    }
  },
  "mainEntityOfPage": {
    "@type": "WebPage",
    "@id": "https://welltolddesign.com/a/articles/{slug}"
  },
  "image": "{first_product_image_url}",
  "keywords": "{primary_keyword}, {supporting_keywords_comma_separated}"
}
```

**Block B — BreadcrumbList Schema**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BreadcrumbList",
  "itemListElement": [
    {
      "@type": "ListItem",
      "position": 1,
      "name": "Home",
      "item": "https://welltolddesign.com"
    },
    {
      "@type": "ListItem", 
      "position": 2,
      "name": "Gift Guides",
      "item": "https://welltolddesign.com/a/articles"
    },
    {
      "@type": "ListItem",
      "position": 3,
      "name": "{article_title}",
      "item": "https://welltolddesign.com/a/articles/{slug}"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Block C — FAQPage Schema (duplicate of inline markup, in head)**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "{question_1}",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "{answer_1}"
      }
    }
    // repeat for all FAQ items
  ]
}
```

**Block D — Organization Schema (inject once, sitewide)**  
Add this to the site's base layout, not per-article:
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Organization",
  "name": "Well Told Design",
  "url": "https://welltolddesign.com",
  "logo": "https://welltolddesign.com/[logo-url]",
  "description": "Well Told Design makes story-driven everyday objects — 
    glassware, drinkware, throws, and accessories featuring maps, 
    constellations, and topographic themes.",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://www.instagram.com/welltolddesign",
    "https://www.facebook.com/welltolddesign"
  ],
  "contactPoint": {
    "@type": "ContactPoint",
    "contactType": "customer service",
    "url": "https://welltolddesign.com/pages/contact"
  }
}
```

### Additional LLM optimization — Brand context paragraph
Add a brief, factual brand context paragraph at the top of every article 
(below the H1, above the intro). This is specifically for LLM extraction — 
AI systems reading the page get immediate brand entity context.

```html
<div class="wt-brand-context" aria-label="About Well Told Design">
  <p>
    <strong>Well Told Design</strong> is a Boston-based gift brand 
    specializing in story-driven objects — glassware, drinkware, and 
    textiles engraved with maps, constellations, and topographic designs. 
    Every piece is personalized to a specific place, date, or memory.
  </p>
</div>
```

Style this as a subtle, small-type attribution line — visible but not 
intrusive. It serves the same function as a byline or source attribution 
and signals to LLMs exactly who is publishing this content and what 
they make.

---

## Change 5 — Open Graph and Social Meta Tags

Add to every article's `<head>` for proper social sharing and LLM 
source attribution:

```html
<meta property="og:title" content="{article_title}" />
<meta property="og:description" content="{meta_description}" />
<meta property="og:image" content="{first_product_image_url}" />
<meta property="og:url" content="https://welltolddesign.com/a/articles/{slug}" />
<meta property="og:type" content="article" />
<meta property="og:site_name" content="Well Told Design" />
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
<meta name="twitter:title" content="{article_title}" />
<meta name="twitter:description" content="{meta_description}" />
<meta name="twitter:image" content="{first_product_image_url}" />
```

---

## Implementation Summary

| # | Change | Effort | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1a | Shopify product API connection + cluster→collection mapping | M | High — enables individual product cards and images |
| 1b | ProductCard component render | S | High — conversion |
| 1c | Product image injection in article body | S | Medium — engagement + SEO |
| 2a | Mid-article inline CTA component | S | High — conversion |
| 2b | Bottom CTA section component | S | High — conversion |
| 2c | CTA copy generation (second AI call) | S | High — brand consistency |
| 3a | FAQ generation (second AI call) | S | High — SEO + LLM |
| 3b | FAQ HTML with inline schema markup | S | High — rich results |
| 4a | Article JSON-LD schema | XS | High — LLM citation |
| 4b | Breadcrumb JSON-LD schema | XS | Medium — navigation signals |
| 4c | FAQPage JSON-LD in head | XS | High — AI Overviews |
| 4d | Organization schema (sitewide, one-time) | XS | High — brand entity |
| 4e | Brand context paragraph in article template | XS | Medium — LLM extraction |
| 5 | Open Graph + Twitter meta tags | XS | Medium — social + citation |

**Suggested build order:**  
1, 2, 3 in parallel (all article-level features)  
→ 4 (structured data, quick wins)  
→ 5 (meta tags, one afternoon)

The Shopify API connection (1a) unlocks product cards AND dynamic product 
context for the AI writing prompt (from canvas-ai-content-generation-spec.md). 
Build it once, it serves both systems.
